title: Hemispheric Asymmetry
limitation: The brain is not perfectly symmetric; mirroring tracts across the midline introduces anatomical error.
mitigation: Use HCP population-average tract atlases rather than single-subject mirrors; report per-tract confidence intervals.

title: Tract-to-Region Mapping
limitation: DTI streamline endpoints are not natively labeled with cortical region identities.
mitigation: Volumetric Desikan-Killiany atlas lookup with nearest-neighbor fallback for endpoints near gray-matter boundaries.

title: Crossing Fibers
limitation: Single-tensor DTI cannot resolve voxels where tracts cross or kiss, degrading trajectory accuracy.
mitigation: HCP data uses multi-shell CSD to resolve crossings; we fall back to centroid approximation in our own DTI.

title: Channel Overlap
limitation: Large cavities may generate more proposed channels than geometry can fit without collision.
mitigation: Priority placement ordered by deficit magnitude, collision detection, 30% cavity fill cap, 50 µm minimum wall thickness.

title: Registration Error
limitation: Accumulated 5–10 mm error when co-registering EEG source space to DTI to MRI.
mitigation: Channels provide centimeter-scale directional guidance, not millimeter targeting — error budget is within tolerance.

title: Printability
limitation: Consumer FDM printers cannot produce 100 µm hollow channels in soft materials.
mitigation: Demonstrate at 5× scale for proof-of-concept. Clinical deployment requires a bioprinter (e.g., LIFT or DLP-based).

title: No Biological Ground Truth
limitation: NRE is a computational metric; it does not yet prove biological function restoration.
mitigation: NRE demonstrates optimization versus controls. Phase 5 (wet-lab validation with SH-SY5Y neurite tracking) tests biological outcome.

title: Cross-Patient Data
limitation: Public datasets pair EEG and MRI from different patients; direct fusion is impossible.
mitigation: Virtual-lesion analysis applies healthy EEG to atlas lesions. Clinical translation would use same-patient acquisition.

title: Oxygen Diffusion
limitation: Tissue more than ~200 µm from a vessel cannot survive without active perfusion.
mitigation: 80% porosity infill between channels; clinical version will include VEGF-loaded microreservoirs to drive vascular ingrowth.

title: Glial Scar
limitation: Reactive astrocytes form a CSPG-rich scar at cavity boundaries that blocks axonal entry.
mitigation: Channel entry points can be loaded with chondroitinase ABC (ChABC) to digest scar CSPGs locally.

title: Implantation Timing
limitation: The subacute window (2–6 weeks post-stroke) closes before chronic glial scarring, but EEG at that window is noisy.
mitigation: Pipeline is designed for subacute EEG; artifact-robust wPLI and ICA minimize noise; pre-surgical planning latency < 48 hr.

title: EEG Channel Count
limitation: Our own laboratory EEG recordings (8 channels) have insufficient spatial resolution for source localization.
mitigation: Electrode-level connectivity used for our data; 64-channel public datasets used for full source-level analysis.
